title: Combat Formation Management
summary: Maintaining effective formations during active combat.
details: Formations provide mutual support and concentrated firepower. Common formations: line abreast (maximum forward firepower), echelon (good coverage, easy to maintain), wedge (flexible, leader protection). Adjust spacing based on enemy weapons - tighter against single targets, wider against area weapons. When formation breaks due to combat, call for reform at rally point. Damaged ships should fall to protected positions within formation.
annotations: Formations work because they provide overlapping fields of fire.
Rigid formation discipline can be fatal - adapt to situation.
